originalTitle: The young women who fought in Ethiopia's last civil war and don't want to see another one
title: Brave Young Women Hope for Peace in Ethiopia!
content:
Hello, Friends from Africa!

Hello, amazing kids! It's your friendly news reporter here. Today, we're going on a special journey to a wonderful country in Africa called Ethiopia. Ethiopia is a beautiful place with big mountains, ancient cities, and many friendly people. Did you know? Ethiopia is one of the oldest countries in the world! It has a very long history and lots of interesting stories. We are going to talk about some brave young women from Ethiopia. They have a very important message for all of us. Their message is about wanting peace and not wanting fighting. It's a very big wish from their hearts.

What is a "Civil War"?

Sometimes, grown-ups argue, right? But imagine if people from the same country started to fight each other. Not with words, but with weapons. That's what we call a "civil war." It's when people inside one country fight against each other. This kind of fighting is very sad. It makes everyone unhappy and unsafe. A little while ago, there was a civil war in a part of Ethiopia called Tigray. It was a very hard time for many people there. Did you know? "Tigray" is a region in the north of Ethiopia. It has its own special culture and beautiful landscapes.

Brave Young Women Who Fought

During that fighting, some young women had to be very, very brave. They were just like big sisters or aunties. They had to join in the fighting to protect their homes and their families. Imagine having to leave your games and your schoolbooks to go and fight. That's what some of these young women did. They became what we call "fighters" or "soldiers." They did not want to fight. They wanted to go to school, play with friends, and help their families. But they felt they had to protect what they loved during that very difficult time. These women are very strong. They have seen many hard things. They remember everything that happened during the fighting. It makes them very sad to think about it.

How War Hurts People and Places

When there is fighting, many bad things happen. Imagine your school being broken. Imagine your home not being safe anymore. Imagine not being able to play outside because it's too dangerous. That's what happened in Tigray. The fighting "destroyed their lives." This means it made their lives very, very hard. Their schools were closed. Their homes were damaged. Many people had to leave their homes to find a safe place. These young women saw their towns and villages get hurt. They saw their friends and family suffer. It was a time full of sadness and worry. They lost many things that were important to them, like a normal, happy life. Did you know? When children cannot go to school because of war, they miss out on learning. This is why peace is so important, so all children can learn and grow!

Why They Want Peace So Much

Now, the fighting has stopped for a little while. And these brave young women have a very, very strong message. They say, "We do not want any more fighting!" They want peace. They want peace more than anything else. What is peace? Peace means no fighting. It means everyone is safe. It means children can go to school. It means families can live happily together. It means people can plant food and build new homes. These women remember how much pain the war brought. They remember how their lives were turned upside down. They don't want any child or any family to go through that again. They dream of a future where there is only peace in Ethiopia. A future where everyone can play, learn, and grow up without fear. They want their country to be happy and strong.

What Happens When There is No Peace?

Sometimes, people get worried. They have "fears." Right now, some grown-ups are a bit worried that the fighting "could resume." This means they are scared that the fighting might start again. If fighting starts again, it means more sadness. It means more schools might be closed. More homes might be broken. More children might not be safe. And that is why these brave women are speaking out. They are saying, "Please, let's keep the peace! Let's not let the fighting start again." They know how much it hurts. They know how much damage it causes to people and to the land. Did you know? When a country has peace, it can grow stronger. People can build new things, plant more food, and help everyone live better lives.

Hoping for a Peaceful Future

These young women are heroes. They fought for their home, and now they are fighting for peace with their voices. They want to make sure that no other young person has to go through what they did. They want to see all children in Ethiopia laughing and playing. They want to see schools full of happy learners. They want to see families together and safe. Let's all hope with these brave women for lasting peace in Ethiopia. Peace is the best gift we can all have. It makes our communities strong, our families happy, and our future bright. We wish for a bright and peaceful future for all children in Ethiopia and across Africa!
summary: Brave young women in Ethiopia who fought in a past war are now asking for peace, hoping that no more fighting will ever happen again and that children can live safely.
whyMatters: This is important because when there is peace, children can go to school, play safely, and grow up happy, instead of being scared or having their homes broken by fighting.

originalTitle: Conservative Anglicans pull back from electing rival to Archbishop of Canterbury
title: Conservative Anglicans pull back from electing rival to Archbishop of Canterbury
content: The group had said it would choose a new leader with the same title as Sarah Mullally, the first female head of the Church.

originalTitle: Caf postpones Wafcon 2026 just 12 days before kick-off
title: Big African Football Games Are Moving! Let's Find Out Why!
content:
Hi everyone! It's your friendly kid reporter from Africa here, ready to share some big news! Grab your footballs and get ready to listen, because we have something important to talk about. It’s about a very special football party for girls and women across Africa!

What is WAFCON? Fun Football Time!

Imagine a huge party where all the best girl and women footballers from different countries in Africa come together. They kick a ball, score goals, and have so much fun! This special party is called the Women's Africa Cup of Nations. We call it WAFCON for short, like a secret club name! It’s super exciting because these amazing women play with all their heart and show us how strong and talented they are. Did you know? Football is the most popular sport in Africa! Lots of kids just like you love to kick a ball around every day! Every two years, these super athletes gather to play. They wear their bright team jerseys and run really fast. They try their best to win a big, shiny trophy. It's like a grand competition where everyone cheers very loudly! The fans come with colourful flags and sing happy songs. It's a wonderful time to celebrate African talent and teamwork.

A Little Surprise: The Games Are Moving!

Now for the big news! The next WAFCON games were going to happen really, really soon, in a beautiful country called Morocco. In just about 12 days, the games were supposed to start! Imagine getting ready for your birthday party, counting down the days, and then hearing it's going to be on a different day. That’s a bit like what happened with WAFCON. The grown-ups who organize the games have decided to move them to a later time. This is called 'postponing' the games. It doesn't mean the games are cancelled forever. Oh no! It just means they will happen at a different time, a little bit later than planned. So, we will have to wait a little bit longer to see our favourite teams play. It’s like when you’re building a big sandcastle, and you need a little more time to make it perfect before you show it to everyone. The WAFCON games are like that sandcastle – they want to make sure it's absolutely perfect for everyone!

Why Do Things Get Postponed?

Sometimes, things need a little more time to become super, super special. When a big event like WAFCON is planned, there are so many things to get ready. Think about it: you need big stadiums where everyone can sit, soft green grass for the players to run on, and places for all the teams to stay. You also need to make sure all the lights are working, and the yummy food is ready for everyone! Maybe the people in charge need more time to make sure everything is just right. They want the fields to be super smooth and safe for all the players. Or maybe they want to make sure all the teams from different African countries can travel easily and be there for the games. It’s all about making the biggest and best football party ever! It's a chance to make the next WAFCON even bigger and better than before! More time means more chances to plan surprises, maybe even bigger flags, or new songs for the fans to sing. So, while we wait, we can get even more excited!

Our Amazing Host Country: Morocco!

Remember I told you the games were going to be in Morocco? Morocco is a wonderful country in North Africa. It has sunny beaches, amazing mountains, and very friendly people. They were so excited to welcome all the teams and fans from across Africa. They had started getting everything ready for the big football party. Did you know? Morocco is famous for its delicious mint tea and colourful markets where you can find many interesting things! It's a country full of magic and history. Even though the games are moved, Morocco will still be the place where WAFCON happens. They will just get a little more time to make their welcome even warmer and brighter! We can still look forward to visiting Morocco and cheering for our teams there when the new dates are announced.

Superstars of African Women's Football!

Our African women footballers are true superheroes! They train really hard every day. They run, they kick, they practice fancy moves with the ball. They are strong, brave, and never give up. These players inspire so many young girls and boys to chase their dreams, whether it’s playing football or doing something else they love. They work as a team, helping each other, passing the ball, and celebrating together. Even with this news, they will keep practicing and getting even better. When the games finally happen, they will be ready to show us their amazing skills! We will cheer for them even louder! Did you know? Many African women footballers play for big teams all around the world, showing everyone how talented they are!

What Happens Now? We Wait and Cheer!

So, what do we do now? We wait! But waiting can be fun too. While we wait for the new dates, we can play football with our friends, practice our own kicks, and dream about scoring big goals. We can draw pictures of our favourite players and colours. When the grown-ups tell us the new date for WAFCON, we will all be ready to jump and cheer! It will be an even bigger celebration because we waited for it. Let's keep supporting all the amazing girls and women who play football. They are fantastic, and we can’t wait to see them shine! Until next time, keep playing, keep smiling, and keep cheering for Africa! Bye for now!
summary: The big African football games for women, called WAFCON, that were supposed to start soon in Morocco have been moved to a later date, but they are not cancelled, just postponed to make them even more special.
whyMatters: This matters because WAFCON is a super fun and important football party for girls and women across Africa, and even though we have to wait a little longer, it means the games will be even better when they finally happen.
